body.title: CC-side announce_mcp.py adapter — exposes announce protocol as MCP tools
body.result: AnnounceMcpServer ships: 3 MCP-style tools (announce_tool, manifest_tool, check_for_invalidate_tool) returning JSON-friendly {ok, ...} dicts. Singleton DatacenterClient. Identity defaults from hostname + pid. Errors returned as data, not exceptions. Wiring into .mcp.json is slice 5. 9 new tests; 219 passed. agent_datacenter commit 0552d7c.
body.description:
CC sessions are clients of the datacenter just like Igor. This ticket ships the CC-side adapter: a small MCP server module (announce_mcp.py) that exposes three tools — datacenter_announce, datacenter_manifest, datacenter_check_for_invalidate — backed by a singleton DatacenterClient. CC invokes these via Claude Code's MCP layer; the tools return JSON-friendly dict shapes. Module is added to agent_datacenter (consistent placement with the rest of the announce protocol) but is wired by Claude Code via .mcp.json — that .mcp.json entry is slice 5 work, this ticket only ships the adapter module itself.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/announce_mcp.py (NEW), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/announce/__init__.py (export AnnounceMcpServer), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/tests/test_announce_mcp_adapter.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** singleton-per-process DatacenterClient (CC sessions are 1:1 with the agent identity); cc.yaml profile drives the announce; tools return JSON-serializable dicts (no dataclass instances over the wire); errors converted to {"error": str} return values rather than exceptions (MCP convention); identity envelope built at module import time using socket.gethostname() + os.getpid() to match Igor's pattern.
**Scope boundary:** in — AnnounceMcpServer wrapper class with three tool methods (announce, manifest, check_for_invalidate); idempotent connect; tests covering each tool path. Out — actual MCP server registration (Claude Code's .mcp.json — slice 5), Igor-side reciprocal tools, FastMCP wiring (this is a thin wrapper that any MCP host can drive; FastMCP integration if desired comes later).
**Test plan:** test_adapter_announces_via_tool (boot in-process Skeleton, instantiate AnnounceMcpServer with cc identity, call announce_tool(), assert manifest dict returned with cc agent_id); test_adapter_returns_error_dict_on_unknown_agent (point at non-existent profile, assert {"error": ...} returned, no exception); test_manifest_tool_returns_cached_manifest_dict; test_check_for_invalidate_tool_handles_envelopes; test_singleton_reuse_across_calls (announce + manifest in sequence share the same client).
